beats.activity: Wake up, check sourdough starter, make coffee and toast.
beats.reasoning: Saturday morning ritual: Viktor starts with his starter and coffee before any errands. Starter needs feeding and he'll plan the day around it.

beats.activity: Walk to U-District Farmers Market, browse produce and local vendors.
beats.reasoning: Saturday farmers market is in his neighborhood and fits his sourdough baking passion—he'll source fresh ingredients and enjoy the local scene before crowds build.
Short walk back to U-District home with groceries; overcast weather makes this pleasant and he's got dough to tend to.

beats.activity: Bake sourdough: shape dough, prepare loaves, begin baking.
beats.reasoning: Saturday is his main baking day; he's meticulous and will spend 2+ hours on shaping, scoring, and starting the oven—this is his zen time after a work week.

beats.activity: Finish baking, cool loaves on rack, tidy kitchen.
beats.reasoning: Final baking phase and cleanup; loaves come out of the oven, he'll let them cool while prepping lunch.

beats.activity: Make lunch: fresh bread, cheese, and local greens from the market.
beats.reasoning: Viktor enjoys eating well and values his time; he'll sit down with his fresh sourdough and market ingredients rather than rush out.

beats.activity: Rest, read the news, check expense spreadsheets.
beats.reasoning: Saturday afternoon downtime; Viktor is meticulous and will review his spending, relax before the afternoon. The Mariners game doesn't appeal to him (crowds, SoDo chaos)—he prefers intimate time or solo pursuits.

beats.activity: Drive to Green Lake for an afternoon walk and to scout fly-fishing conditions.
beats.reasoning: Overcast, 64°F—perfect weather for a walk and outdoor observation. Viktor values fly-fishing and will scout the lake; it's close enough to U-District and avoids downtown crowds.

beats.activity: Walk around Green Lake, enjoy the overcast afternoon, check water clarity.
beats.reasoning: Peaceful Saturday afternoon walk; he's not rushing and will take his time observing conditions and enjoying solitude rather than joining crowds at the Mariners game.
Return home before evening; allows buffer time and avoids any SoDo/downtown traffic from the Mariners game.

beats.activity: Prep dinner, cook a simple meal, feed starter for evening, tidy and organize.
beats.reasoning: Late afternoon at home: he'll tend his starter, prep a meal (likely using fresh bread from the morning bake), and keep the rhythm of his meticulous weekend routine.

beats.activity: Dinner and evening relaxation: read, listen to music, fold more dough if needed.
beats.reasoning: Saturday evening wind-down; Viktor values quiet time with his hands (dough work) and reflection after a day of activity. Low energy yesterday (45/100) means he's protecting his peace.
12-hour sleep span (overcast Saturday, low energy carry-over, rewarding day of baking and outdoors); he'll wake refreshed Sunday morning.

diary:
Saturday started slow and quiet, which is exactly what I needed. Got my sourdough starter going first thing—there's something grounding about that routine, the smell of it bubbling on the counter with my coffee. Walked over to the U-District market while the morning was still cool; picked up some gorgeous greens and a wedge of that sharp cheddar I like. The vendors were chatty, but the overcast kept crowds thin.

Back home, I shaped the loaves and let them rise. The whole apartment smelled like yeast and possibility by mid-morning. Had a proper lunch on that fresh bread—simple, but it hit different when you've made it yourself. Felt restless after, so I drove out to Green Lake to check the water. It's been murky lately, but today it looked clearer. Walked the loop, didn't fish, just needed to be outside and think.

Rest of the day was kitchen time—prepped dinner, fed the starter again, tidied up. Quiet evening with music and a book. Felt settled.
thought_process: Today was about rhythm and self-care, which I don't always give myself permission to take. I deliberately avoided checking work emails—Mariners game traffic would've been a headache anyway, and I wasn't in the mood for crowds. The farmers market scratched an itch I've had all week; I wanted something tactile and real after dealing with permit paperwork and subcontractor drama. Sourdough baking fills the same space for me—it's tangible progress, immediate results. Green Lake was strategic too; I've been meaning to scout it properly for summer fishing, but honestly, I just needed to walk and clear my head. The overcast weather suited my mood perfectly. By evening, I was content to stay in. At 55, I'm learning that Saturdays don't have to be about going out or doing; sometimes the best day is one spent mastering small, quiet rituals at home.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
